title: Subscribe to a podcast found via keyword search
preconditions: App launched and on Home tab
Library empty or known baseline subscription count recorded
steps: Wait for results list to render
Tap the first matching podcast result row
On the podcast detail screen, tap the Subscribe button
assertions: Search results render at least one podcast cell with title and artwork
Subscribe button toggles to a subscribed/Unsubscribe state immediately (reactive, no manual refresh)
The subscribed podcast appears in Library without a reload
Library subscription count increased by exactly 1

title: Subscribe by pasting a raw RSS feed URL
preconditions: A valid known RSS feed URL is available (e.g. a public podcast feed)
steps: Type or paste a full https RSS feed URL
Wait for the feed to be parsed and a single podcast result to render
assertions: App detects the input is a URL and fetches/parses the feed rather than doing keyword search
A podcast detail is shown with correct title and at least one episode listed
Subscribe succeeds and the podcast appears in Library

title: Unsubscribe a podcast and confirm removal
steps: Tap a subscribed podcast to open its detail
Tap Unsubscribe (or swipe-to-delete on the library row)
Confirm the action if a confirmation dialog appears
assertions: The podcast is removed from Library reactively (no manual refresh)
Subscription count decreased by exactly 1
Re-opening the podcast from search shows the Subscribe (not Unsubscribe) state

title: Tap an episode and confirm playback starts under budget
preconditions: At least one subscribed podcast with episodes
steps: Tap an episode row to open episode detail (or tap its play button)
Observe the mini-player / now-playing UI appear
Wait until the elapsed time begins advancing
assertions: Playback starts and the elapsed time counter advances within the budget
Mini-player appears with episode title and artwork
Play button state flips to Pause
Audio session is active (now-playing populated)

title: Pause and resume keeps position
preconditions: An episode is actively playing past 0:05
steps: Note the current elapsed time T
Confirm elapsed time has not advanced
assertions: On pause the elapsed time freezes at ~T and the button shows Play
While paused the counter does not advance
On resume playback continues from ~T (no restart to 0:00)
No audible glitch / no FFI state desync between kernel and UI

title: Skip 15s forward and back adjusts position correctly
preconditions: An episode is playing at a position with > 30s remaining and > 30s elapsed
assertions: After skip-forward, position advances by approximately the configured interval (default 15s, within +/-1s)
After skip-back, position decreases by approximately the configured interval
T3 is approximately equal to T (round-trip returns near original)
Skipping does not pause playback

title: Scrub the progress slider to seek
preconditions: An episode is loaded in the full player with known duration
steps: Drag the progress slider thumb to roughly the midpoint
assertions: Elapsed time jumps to ~50% of duration
Audio resumes playing from the seeked position (if it was playing)
Slider position and numeric elapsed label stay consistent (no drift between them)

title: Change playback speed and confirm it applies
steps: Observe elapsed-time advance rate over 4 wall-clock seconds
assertions: The speed control label updates to 1.5x
Elapsed time advances ~6s over 4 wall-clock seconds (1.5x rate, within tolerance)
No audio dropout when changing speed

title: Playback speed persists across app restart
preconditions: App can be force-quit and relaunched
steps: Set playback speed to a non-default value (e.g. 1.5x) via player or Settings
Start playing any episode and open the player
assertions: Playback speed control shows the previously-set 1.5x value after relaunch
New playback honors 1.5x without re-setting it
Setting survived the kernel/preferences persistence round-trip

title: Episode with chapters shows chapter list
preconditions: A subscribed episode known to contain chapter markers is available
steps: Locate the Chapters section / open the chapters view
Verify chapter titles and start timestamps are listed
assertions: Chapters render in ascending timestamp order
Each chapter shows a title and a start time
Chapter count matches the feed's chapter data

title: Tapping a chapter seeks playback to its timestamp
preconditions: A chaptered episode is loaded and chapters list is visible
steps: Tap a chapter that starts later in the episode
Return to the player and read elapsed time
assertions: Elapsed time jumps to within +/-2s of the tapped chapter's start timestamp
The player highlights/labels the current chapter as the tapped one
Playback continues from the new position

title: Skip segments (intro/ads) are auto-skipped during playback
preconditions: An episode with defined skip segments is available
steps: Seek to just before a skip segment's start
Let playback run through the segment boundary
Observe elapsed time across the boundary
assertions: Playback jumps over the skip segment automatically
Elapsed time lands at or after the segment end without stopping
A skip indication is shown to the user (toast/marker)

title: Download an episode and confirm progress + completion
preconditions: A subscribed podcast with a downloadable episode
assertions: Download progress UI appears and advances from 0 toward 100% (reactive updates from kernel)
On completion the control changes to a Downloaded/Delete state
The episode is marked as available offline in its row badge
No stuck-at-0% or stuck-at-99% hang

title: Downloaded episode plays with network disabled
assertions: Playback starts from local file with no network
Elapsed time advances normally
No buffering/error state shown for the offline file

title: Delete a downloaded episode removes local file and updates UI
steps: Tap Delete download (or swipe to delete in a Downloads list)
assertions: The Downloaded badge is removed reactively
Control reverts to Download state
Storage usage figure (if shown in Settings) decreases
Episode metadata and play position are preserved (only the file is removed)

title: Library remains accessible with no network
preconditions: Several podcasts subscribed and previously loaded
assertions: Library renders cached subscriptions without network
Podcast detail shows previously-fetched episode list from cache
No blocking error screen; offline state is communicated gracefully
App does not crash or hang waiting on the relay/network

title: Add multiple episodes to the queue
preconditions: At least 3 episodes available across subscriptions
steps: Add episode A to queue (long-press or context action -> Add to Queue)
assertions: Queue shows A, B, C in the order added
Queue count badge equals 3
Each entry shows correct title and source podcast

title: Reorder queue via drag
preconditions: Queue has at least 3 items A, B, C in that order
steps: Drag item C above item A
assertions: New queue order is C, A, B
Reordering persists when leaving and returning to the queue
Kernel queue state matches displayed order (no UI-only reorder)

title: Remove an item from the queue
steps: Swipe-to-delete (or remove action) on the second item
assertions: The removed item disappears reactively
Queue count decreases by 1
Remaining items keep their relative order

title: Queue auto-advances to next episode on completion
preconditions: Queue has at least 2 items A then B
steps: Seek to within a few seconds of A's end
assertions: On A completion, B begins playing automatically
A is removed from (or marked played in) the queue
Now-playing metadata updates to B
No gap where playback stalls indefinitely

title: Audio continues when app is backgrounded
steps: Send the app to background (home / scene phase change)
assertions: Audio keeps playing while backgrounded (T2 ~ T + 5s)
On return, UI elapsed time matches actual playback position (no kernel/UI desync after scene-phase change)
Now-playing info remains populated

title: Control Center / lock screen controls drive playback
preconditions: Remote command center supported in the test environment
steps: Trigger remote pause command (Control Center toggle)
assertions: Remote pause pauses in-app and updates the in-app button state
Remote play resumes
Remote skip moves position by the configured interval
Now-playing elapsed/duration metadata stays accurate

title: CarPlay scene shows podcasts and controls
preconditions: At least one subscribed podcast with episodes
steps: Browse to a podcast and select an episode
assertions: CarPlay lists subscribed podcasts and episodes
Selecting an episode starts playback
Transport controls work and reflect state
No crash on CarPlay connect/disconnect scene transitions

title: Auto-download rule downloads new episodes for a podcast
steps: Open Settings -> Auto-download (or the podcast's settings)
Enable auto-download for new episodes for that podcast
Trigger a feed refresh that surfaces a new episode (or simulate one)
assertions: The auto-download toggle persists
A newly-arrived episode begins downloading automatically without manual tap
Completed auto-download is marked offline-available
Rule is scoped to the chosen podcast (others not auto-downloaded)

title: Auto-download settings persist across restart
preconditions: Auto-download configured with at least one non-default rule
steps: Configure auto-download rules (e.g. limit to 3 most recent, wifi-only)
assertions: All configured rules display their previously-set values
wifi-only / episode-limit options survive persistence
No defaults silently restored

title: Import an OPML file adds podcasts to library
preconditions: A valid OPML file with several feeds is available to the app (Files/share sheet)
steps: Open Settings -> Import (or trigger OPML import flow)
Wait for feeds to be parsed and added
assertions: All feeds in the OPML are added as subscriptions (count increases by the OPML feed count, minus any duplicates)
Imported podcasts render with titles and artwork in Library
Malformed/duplicate entries are handled gracefully (no crash, deduped)

title: Export current library as OPML
steps: Trigger export and capture the share sheet / saved file
assertions: An OPML document is produced containing an outline entry per subscribed podcast
Each entry includes the feed xmlUrl and title
Exported file is valid XML/OPML (re-importable)

title: View or create a Nostr keypair / profile
preconditions: Either no key yet (create path) or an existing key (view path)
steps: If no key exists, tap Create/Generate keypair
assertions: A valid npub (bech32, npub1... prefix) is generated/shown
The private key is stored securely (Keychain) and not displayed in plaintext by default
Identity persists across relaunch (same npub)
Profile metadata fields are editable and save

title: Publish a podcast via NIP-F4 to the relay
assertions: A NIP-F4 event is signed with the user's key and accepted by the relay (OK message)
Publishing is reactive (status updates via NDKSwift sequences, not polling)
Success is reflected in the UI
On relay failure, a clear error is shown without crashing

title: Discover a podcast published over Nostr
preconditions: At least one NIP-F4 podcast event exists on the relay
steps: Open the Nostr discovery / Friends feed
Wait for relay subscription to deliver events
assertions: Discovered podcasts populate reactively as relay events arrive (no manual poll)
A discovered podcast can be opened and subscribed
Event metadata (title, feed) maps correctly into the podcast model

title: Generate a transcript for an episode via AI
preconditions: Ollama reachable at localhost:11434 with required model
assertions: Transcript generation starts and streams/updates without blocking the UI
Final transcript renders with readable text and (if supported) timestamps
Transcript is cached/persisted and shown immediately on re-open
Graceful error if Ollama is unreachable

title: Generate an episode summary via AI
preconditions: An episode with available transcript or description
steps: Wait for the summary to generate
assertions: A concise summary is produced and rendered
Summary is persisted and re-displayed on re-open without regeneration
UI remains responsive during generation

title: Ask the agent a question about an episode
preconditions: An episode with transcript context available
steps: Open AgentChat from the episode context
Type a question about the episode content
Send and wait for the streamed response
assertions: The agent responds with content grounded in the episode/transcript
Response streams token-by-token without freezing the UI
Conversation history is retained within the session

title: Create a clipping from the currently-playing episode
assertions: A clipping is created with the correct episode reference and timestamp range
The clipping appears in the Clippings list
Playing the clipping seeks to the captured range

title: Bookmark a position and view it later
steps: Tap Bookmark at the current position
assertions: Bookmark is created with the episode and timestamp
It appears in the Bookmarks list reactively
Tapping it opens the episode and seeks to the bookmarked time

title: Save AI / credentials settings and confirm persistence
steps: Enter or change a credential/endpoint (e.g. Ollama base URL or an API key field)
assertions: The saved value is shown after relaunch
Secret fields are stored in Keychain and masked, not plaintext in defaults
Changing the endpoint actually routes subsequent AI calls to the new endpoint

title: Episode play position is remembered across restart
preconditions: An episode played to a non-trivial position (e.g. 2:00)
steps: Play an episode to ~2:00 and pause
assertions: The episode shows a resume point at ~2:00 (within a few seconds)
Tapping Play resumes from ~2:00, not 0:00
Progress is reflected in the episode row (partial-played indicator)

title: Mark episode played / unplayed updates state
preconditions: A subscribed episode that is unplayed
assertions: Marking played sets the played indicator and (per settings) may remove from queue/up-next
Marking unplayed restores the unplayed state and resets/keeps position per app rules
State changes are reactive across the episode list and detail

title: Large library (50+ podcasts) loads under budget
preconditions: 50+ podcasts subscribed (seed via OPML import or fixture)
steps: Measure time from tab tap to fully-rendered list
Scroll the full list rapidly top-to-bottom
assertions: Library renders within the budget
Scrolling stays smooth with no sustained blank cells / no visible jank
Artwork loads lazily without blocking the main thread
Memory does not balloon during scroll

title: Player open/close and play/pause transitions are smooth
steps: Rapidly open and dismiss the full player 5 times
assertions: Each play/pause toggle is honored with no missed taps and no kernel/UI state divergence
Open/close animation does not drop frames noticeably
Rapid episode switching does not leave stale now-playing metadata or orphaned audio
No crash from rapid FFI state mutation

title: State change on one screen reflects on another reactively
preconditions: An episode visible in both a list and a detail/player simultaneously reachable
steps: Start downloading an episode from its detail
Navigate back to the episode list while download is in progress
Observe the list row's download indicator
Start playback and navigate to a different tab, then to the mini-player
assertions: Download progress on the list row updates live from the same kernel state (single source of truth)
Play state shown in mini-player, list, and detail are all consistent
No screen shows stale state requiring a manual refresh (push-seam reactivity working)

title: Search handles empty query and no-results gracefully
steps: Type a nonsense string unlikely to match (e.g. "zxqwv-no-such-podcast-9911")
assertions: Empty query does not crash and shows a sensible empty/initial state
No-results query shows an explicit empty-results message, not a spinner forever
Clearing the field restores the default search state

title: First-launch onboarding completes and is not shown again
preconditions: Fresh install / app data erased so onboarding has not been completed
steps: Step through the onboarding screens to completion
assertions: Onboarding flow renders on first launch
Completing it lands the user on Home/Library
On subsequent relaunch onboarding is NOT shown again (completion flag persisted)

title: What's New screen appears after update and dismisses permanently
preconditions: App version that triggers the What's New screen on first launch of that version
steps: Launch the app for the first time at the new version
assertions: What's New is shown once for the new version
After dismissal it does not reappear on relaunch of the same version
Dismiss-state is keyed to version and persists

title: Voice STT captures a spoken command/query
preconditions: Audio input available in the test environment
assertions: Transcribed text appears for the spoken input
The transcription drives the intended action (e.g. search) if wired
Graceful handling if mic permission denied or provider unreachable

title: Voice TTS reads text aloud
steps: From a TTS-enabled surface (e.g. summary read-aloud), trigger Speak
assertions: Synthesized speech plays back
TTS playback respects pause/stop controls
TTS does not conflict with / corrupt the episode audio session state

title: Rapid subscribe/unsubscribe cycling stays consistent (FFI stress)
preconditions: A podcast detail open with a Subscribe button
steps: Stop and read the final button state
assertions: Final UI state matches actual kernel subscription state (even/odd taps reconcile correctly)
Library reflects the true final state (present iff subscribed)
No crash, no duplicate library entries, no orphaned kernel subscription from rapid FFI mutation

title: Opening a podcast/episode deep link routes correctly
preconditions: A deep link / shared Nostr link to a podcast or episode is available
steps: Trigger the deep link while app is cold (not running)
Then trigger another deep link while app is already running
assertions: Cold-start deep link routes to the correct podcast/episode after launch
Warm deep link navigates to the correct destination without losing player state
Invalid/unresolvable link shows a graceful error, not a crash

title: Pull-to-refresh surfaces new episodes
preconditions: A subscribed podcast known to have a fresh episode not yet loaded
steps: Note current episode count / newest episode
Pull to refresh (or trigger refresh action)
assertions: New episodes are fetched and prepended reactively
Episode count increases / newest episode updates if the feed has new items
Refresh indicator clears when done; no infinite spinner
Read/played and download state of existing episodes is preserved across refresh
